target.name: Dyna Robotics
target.founder: Lindon Gao, York Yang, Jason Ma

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Dyna Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $120M to date. The valuation universe for this stage includes one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers in Robotics & Manufacturing, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Given the limited peer set at identical stage and valuation tier, peer-median methodology was not applicable—only two adjacent-stage comparables were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold for reliable triangulation. The stage-progression method proved viable, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $120M total raised as a valuation proxy. This yielded an implied post-Series A valuation of $240M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $204M to $276M, with a median point of $240M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single applicable methodology rather than true triangulation across multiple independent approaches. A peer-median check becomes feasible only with additional same-stage private datapoints or closer public comp alignment. Until then, the $240M figure should be treated as indicative rather than definitive, and revisited as additional comparable transaction data emerges in the robotics subsector.
